articles.title: CCS Rules | Temporary Status Casual Labourer Entitled To Pension Even Without Formal Regularisation : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that long-serving casual workers in government who perform permanent and continuous work are entitled to pensionary benefits even without formal regularisation. The Court held that such workers qualify for benefits under the Central Civil Services (Temporary Service) Rules, 1965. This decision impacts temporary government servants and sets a precedent for worker welfare in India.

articles.title: Odisha Makes Electric Vehicles Mandatory for Official Use; New Procurement Rules Take Effect
articles.summary: The Odisha government has made it mandatory for official use vehicles to be electric, with new procurement rules now in effect. This decision aims to address rising fuel costs amid global energy price fluctuations and geopolitical uncertainties. The move aligns with broader efforts to promote sustainable transportation in government operations.
articles.raw_summary: The decision comes at a time of fluctuating global energy prices and growing concerns over fuel costs, exacerbated by geopolitical uncertainties in several parts of the world.

articles.title: Civil Courts can order police assistance to implement ad interim injunction under S. 151 CPC: Himachal Pradesh HC
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court ruled that civil courts can direct police assistance to enforce ad interim injunctions under Section 151 of the Civil Procedure Code (CPC). The ruling ensures that if such injunctions are not implemented properly, their purpose would be defeated. The decision was made in the context of a case where the plaintiff had established prima facie entitlement to protection and irreparable loss.
articles.raw_summary: The plaintiff had placed on record the necessary permissions and had established a prima facie entitlement to protection, balance of convenience and irreparable loss. Therefore, in case the ad interim injunction was not implemented in its letter and spirit, then the very purpose of passing such an order would be defeated. The post Civil Courts can order police assistance to implement ad interim injunction under S. 151 CPC: Himachal Pradesh HC appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: India, Oman trade pact to come into force from June 1; Check what's inside
articles.summary: India and Oman's Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement, CEPA, begins on June 1. This pact offers Indian exports 100% duty-free access to Oman. Service professionals gain new opportunities. This agreement strengthens India's Middle East trade ties.

articles.title: Kerala HC Allows ITC Claim as Section 16(5) Overrides Section 16(4) Time Limit
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that Section 16(5) of the GST law takes precedence over Section 16(4), allowing a taxpayer to claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) even after the prescribed deadline. The court set aside the denial of ITC since the taxpayer had filed returns before the cut-off date. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of GST provisions and impacts how tax authorities process ITC claims. The ruling is significant for businesses and tax compliance under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the non-obstante clause in Section 16(5) prevails over Section 16(4). Since the returns were filed before the prescribed cut-off date, the ITC denial was set aside. The post Kerala HC Allows ITC Claim as Section 16(5) Overrides Section 16(4) Time Limit appeared first on TaxGuru .

They emphasized the urgency of the matter and requested immediate action from central agencies, including the Home Secretary and Intelligence Bureau. The appeal underscores concerns over civil liberties and security in the region, potentially prompting policy intervention.
articles.raw_summary: Mr Rio also informed that he spoke to the union home secretary and the Director of Intelligence Bureau, citing the urgency and sensitivity of the matter and requesting immediate and coordinated action by all concerned agencies.

articles.title: ITAT Restores Appeal as Taxpayer Could Not Respond to SCN Due to Illness
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) restored an appeal after dismissing it as time-barred because the taxpayer could not respond to a show-cause notice (SCN) due to illness. Medical records supported the taxpayer's health issues. The ITAT allowed the appeal to be decided on merits. This ruling highlights the judiciary's role in addressing procedural delays caused by genuine hardships.
articles.raw_summary: The appeal was dismissed as time-barred due to a 43-day delay in filing. ITAT held that medical records substantiated the assessee’s health issues and restored the appeal for decision on merits. articles.title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO cannot Reduce FMV Backed by Registered Valuer Report
articles.summary: The Surat Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that for assessment years before 2013-14, the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) lacked authority under Section 55A to reduce the fair market value (FMV) of assets based on a registered valuer’s report. As a result, the tribunal deleted the Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) addition imposed by tax authorities. The decision clarifies the scope of DVO’s powers in valuation disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Surat ITAT held that for assessment years prior to AY 2013-14, the DVO had no authority under Section 55A to reduce the fair market value adopted by an assessee based on a registered valuer’s report. The resulting LTCG addition was therefore deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO cannot Reduce FMV Backed by Registered Valuer Report appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Deletes Section 272A(1)(d) Penalty as Acute Depression Constituted Reasonable Cause
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Lucknow ruled that acute depression constitutes a reasonable cause for non-compliance with tax notices, leading to the deletion of a penalty under Section 272A(1)(d). The tribunal relied on Section 273B, which exempts penalties for genuine reasonable causes. This decision sets a precedent for how mental health conditions may be considered in tax compliance cases.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Lucknow held that acute depression prevented timely compliance with statutory notices and amounted to a reasonable cause under Section 273B. Consequently, the penalty under Section 272A(1)(d) was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes Section 272A(1)(d) Penalty as Acute Depression Constituted Reasonable Cause appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Quashes Section 154 Rectification as Survey Income Character Was a Debatable Issue
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that income disclosed during a survey cannot be reclassified from business income to Section 69A income through rectification proceedings under Section 154. The ITAT held that a debatable issue cannot be treated as a mistake apparent from the record, setting a precedent for tax disputes involving income characterization. The ruling impacts how tax authorities may approach rectification requests in similar cases.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a reassessment order against a real estate company was invalid because the Assessing Officer relied solely on an Investigation Wing report without conducting independent verification. articles.title: RBI launches the Survey on Foreign Liabilities and Assets of Mutual Funds: 2025-26 round
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank hereby launches the 2025-26 round of its annual survey on  Foreign Liabilities and Assets of Mutual Funds . The survey collects the information from mutual funds on their external financial liabilities and assets as at end-March of the financial year 2025-26. The aggregated survey results are disseminated in the public domain and are used in the compilation of India s external sector statistics. Mutual funds are required to fill the survey schedule, which is available on the RBI

This exposes critical admin functions such as exporting data and generating appointment letters. The issue follows similar concerns raised about the CBSE marking system's security flaws. The findings raise questions about the government's data protection measures for examination systems.

articles.summary: ITAT Chennai held that there is no provision under the Income-tax Act allowing substitution of the actual cost of land with its fair market value while computing deduction under Section 80-IB(10). articles.title: ITAT Allows Subsidiary Investment Write-Off as Business Loss as Investment Was Made for Commercial Expediency
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a subsidiary's investment can be written off as a business loss if the investment was made for commercial reasons. The tribunal remanded a related issue regarding the application of Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) rates to dividends paid to non-resident shareholders due to missing documents like Tax Residency Certificates (TRCs). The decision impacts how businesses account for investments and dividends in their tax filings.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT remanded the issue of applying DTAA rates to dividends paid to non-resident shareholders because crucial documents such as TRCs and related records were not available on record. The post ITAT Allows Subsidiary Investment Write-Off as Business Loss as Investment Was Made for Commercial Expediency appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Hindu Succession Act | After Intestate Succession, One Co-Heir Cannot Alienate Shares Of Others Acting As Karta : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that after intestate succession, one co-heir cannot act as the 'karta' (manager) and alienate the shares of other co-heirs. The Court held that co-heirs inherit property as tenants-in-common, and the concept of kartaship does not apply in such cases.
articles.raw_summary: The co-heirs will inherit the property as tenants-in-common and the concept of “kartaship” doesn't apply, the Court held.

articles.title: Sebi proposes relief for road InvITs, may allow maintenance debt to be added back in cash flow calculations
articles.summary: Sebi has proposed allowing road InvITs to add back major maintenance expenses funded by external debt when calculating Net Distributable Cash Flow (NDCF). This move aims to address industry concerns about reduced distributable cash, as these expenses, though crucial for asset longevity, cannot be capitalized. The proposal, following industry representations, requires unitholder approval and extensive disclosures.

The proposal, following industry representations, requires unitholder approval and extensive disclosures.

articles.title: GDP growth likely eased in January-March quarter on softer external demand
articles.summary: India's economic growth is projected to have eased to 7.2% in Q1 2026, as weaker external demand and industrial activity tempered strong government spending and agricultural resilience. Services growth is expected to stay robust, but manufacturing may see slower expansion.

articles.summary: In the second phase of Himachal Pradesh's panchayat elections, voter turnout reached 79.79%, up from 76% in 2021. Women voters surpassed men with an 82.97% turnout compared to 76.69% for men. The higher participation, especially among women, highlights trends in electoral engagement that could influence future governance or policy decisions on inclusive voting practices.

articles.summary: The PM-SVANidhi scheme has significantly boosted street vendor incomes by 20%. Digital transactions by vendors have nearly doubled, reaching over 83%. The scheme provides collateral-free loans, with 1.05 crore loans disbursed. Defaults are low, and many vendors are now accessing loans from other banks, building a permanent credit history. The scheme aims to disburse 1.5 crore loans.
articles.raw_summary: The PM-SVANidhi scheme has significantly boosted street vendor incomes by 20%. Digital transactions by vendors have nearly doubled, reaching over 83%. The scheme provides collateral-free loans, with 1.05 crore loans disbursed. Defaults are low, and many vendors are now accessing loans from other banks, building a permanent credit history. The scheme aims to disburse 1.5 crore loans.

articles.title: US takes step to halt Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms outside China
articles.summary: The US has issued guidance to halt shipments of its advanced AI chips to Chinese firms operating outside China, including subsidiaries in Malaysia. This follows reports that Nvidia's best AI chips were reaching Chinese companies despite US restrictions aimed at limiting China's access to critical semiconductor technology. The move underscores ongoing global tensions over semiconductor trade and AI technology access.
articles.raw_summary: The unexpected guidance suggests the United States' ‌best AI chips ⁠may ⁠have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places like ​Malaysia for almost a year despite broader US efforts to starve Chinese firms of ​semiconductors needed to develop critical AI capabilities.

articles.title: ASUS plans more India-made laptops with Dixon, aims to grow beyond metro cities
articles.summary: Taiwanese laptop maker ASUS expects locally manufactured laptops to account for 30% of its India sales this year, up from 10% last year, as it deepens its partnership with Dixon Technologies. The company is also expanding aggressively beyond metros, betting on smaller cities and towns to drive its next phase of growth in India.

articles.title: ATF for international airlines cut 27%; commercial LPG, 5-kg cylinder prices raised
articles.summary: Domestic airlines will continue paying ₹104,927.18 per kilolitre for Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF), a rate set on April 1 that only partially addressed the required increase. Meanwhile, the price of commercial 5-kg LPG cylinders has been raised. The unchanged ATF pricing since April suggests ongoing regulatory or policy challenges affecting fuel costs for airlines and consumers.
articles.raw_summary: Domestic carriers will continue to pay ₹104,927.18 per kilolitre for ATF, the rate that became applicable on April 1, when only a fourth of the required increase was passed on to airlines. The prices have remained unchanged since then.

articles.title: Government steps up to save fertilisers as 20% of subsidy exhausted in 2 months
articles.summary: The Agriculture Ministry has reduced fertiliser demand for the kharif season due to a forecast of below-normal monsoon and the exhaustion of 20% of the fertiliser subsidy in just two months. This decision aims to manage resource allocation and mitigate potential agricultural disruptions. The move reflects proactive policy adjustments by the government to address anticipated challenges in the farming sector.
articles.raw_summary: Agriculture Ministry lowers fertiliser demand for kharif season in view of forecast of below-normal monsoon

articles.title: India reviews duty sop on Scotch in UK trade deal
articles.summary: India is reviewing tariff concessions on Scotch and other goods in its trade deal with the UK. This action is contingent on the UK addressing India's concerns regarding steel quotas and duties. UK Trade Secretary Peter Kyle is set to meet with India's Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal to resolve this deadlock.

articles.title: Kerala launches statewide cyber safety pledge for students on school reopening day
articles.summary: Kerala Police launched the 'Kid Glove Project' on June 1, when schools reopened, requiring students to take a cyber safety pledge. The initiative aims to educate young internet users about online threats, cyber hygiene, privacy protection, fake news, and responsible digital behavior. The project is part of Kerala's efforts to address growing concerns about digital risks among schoolchildren.
articles.raw_summary: As schools reopen across Kerala on June 1, students will take a cyber safety pledge under the Kerala Police's Kid Glove Project. The initiative seeks to raise awareness about online threats, cyber hygiene, privacy protection, fake news and responsible digital behaviour among young internet users.

articles.summary: New digital marking system is aimed at reducing human errors but many students say it has resulted in wrong grades National outcry has erupted in India after more than 400,000 students have requested copies of their exam papers and answer sheets amid an outcry over marking errors in the country’s most important school-leaving exams. articles.title: Ecommerce players to join hands for the Digital Commerce Coalition

The coalition aims to improve customer experiences, support small businesses and delivery partners, and promote economic growth through collective efforts. It will also serve as a platform for responsible and sustainable growth in the digital commerce sector.
articles.raw_summary: Leading ecommerce players, including Eternal, Meesho, Amazon, Swiggy, and Zepto, have launched the Digital Commerce Coalition. This industry-led platform aims to enhance customer experiences, support small businesses and delivery partners, and foster economic development through collective action. The coalition will also serve as a forum for responsible and future-ready growth.

articles.title: Karnataka Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot accepts Siddaramaiah’s resignation as chief minister
articles.summary: Karnataka Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot accepted Chief Minister Siddaramaiah's resignation. Siddaramaiah stated he was leaving office with a clear conscience and will continue in an acting capacity until a new arrangement is made. The resignation marks a significant shift in the state's political leadership.
articles.raw_summary: Announcing his resignation at a packed press conference on Thursday, the 77-year-old Siddaramaiah asserted nbsp;that he was demitting office with a clean conscience. Siddaramaiah shall continue to function as chief minister till an alternative arrangement is made, Governor said.

articles.title: Google’s Keyword Empire and Business of Borrowed Goodwill
articles.summary: A court ruled that using a registered trademark as a keyword to divert online traffic can constitute trademark infringement. The case highlights that invisible keyword use may attract liability when it exploits a brand’s commercial reputation. The ruling has implications for digital advertising and trademark law in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court ruled that using a registered trademark as a keyword to divert online traffic can amount to trademark infringement. The key takeaway is that invisible keyword use may attract liability when it exploits a brand’s commercial reputation. The post Google s Keyword Empire and Business of Borrowed Goodwill appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GSTAT Upholds Profiteering Finding as Cinema Increased Base Ticket Prices After GST Rate Cut
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) upheld a profiteering finding against cinema operators who increased base ticket prices after a GST rate cut. The tribunal ruled that businesses could not override the statutory requirement to pass on GST rate reduction benefits to consumers. This decision reinforces consumer protection under GST laws and may influence future pricing practices in the entertainment sector.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT held that permissions relating to ticket pricing could not override the statutory requirement to pass on GST rate reduction benefits to consumers. The profiteering demand was sustained. The post GSTAT Upholds Profiteering Finding as Cinema Increased Base Ticket Prices After GST Rate Cut appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ‘Gaddi’ vs private property: The royal inheritance conundrum & how SC settled it with Kapurthala ruling
articles.summary: The Supreme Court settled a 50-year-old property dispute involving princely states like Dholpur, Rampur, and Kapurthala, clarifying the distinction between 'Gaddi' (royal inheritance) and private property. The ruling provides the clearest resolution yet on how royal properties should be inherited and managed. The case involved long-standing legal ambiguities over the rights of former royal families to their ancestral properties.

This raises concerns about wildlife health and conservation policies in India's protected areas.

articles.title: India's chip startups cross from prototype to production
articles.summary: Indian semiconductor startups like Netrasemi, Mindgrove, and Agnit are nearing commercial production, with some already running customer pilots and aiming for large-scale output by late 2027. articles.title: Cotton prices down 3% on import duty suspension, may slide more
articles.summary: India has suspended its 11% import duty on cotton until October. This move has immediately reversed the recent rally in cotton prices, with both bales and yarn seeing a drop. articles.summary: In anticipation of the upcoming sowing season, India is making strategic changes by reducing its fertilizer requirements due to forecasts of insufficient monsoon rainfall. This adjustment means lower demand for urea and DAP, while farmers are encouraged to minimize their reliance on chemical fertilizers, promoting better soil health.

articles.summary: Foreign investors' cumulative net equity investments in India have fallen to their lowest level since 2016 after sustained selling, underscoring weakening appeal for the country's $4.9 trillion stock market. articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

Such repeated appearances, especially in sensitive cases, can cause undue distress to victims of heinous crimes.
